item.title: Hawaiian stilt reclassified from endangered to threatened after 50 years of conservation efforts
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service announced the reclassification of the Aʻeo, or Hawaiian stilt (Himantopus mexicanus knudseni), from endangered to threatened under the Endangered Species Act, a rare and hopeful milestone for a native Hawaiian species. The change, published in the Federal Register alongside a species-specific 4(d) rule, reflects more than 50 years of conservation work that has brought the long-legged waterbird back from the brink. The recovery is documented in a new study led b

item.title: Researchers call for a shift in crop development to tackle hunger, malnutrition, and climate change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Feeding a growing global population is no longer enough. Scientists say the next generation of crops must also deliver better nutrition while withstanding the increasing impacts of climate change.

item.title: Extreme precipitation in Central Asia connected to drought in mainland Southeast Asia
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — In much of Asia, the effects of climate change are felt through an increased frequency of extreme weather events, especially water-related ones. Droughts are becoming more common in mainland Southeast Asia, while periods with unusually large amounts of rainfall are increasing in Central Asia. Rice University's Na Wang and Sylvia Dee were interested in understanding whether these two extremes were related, despite being geographically distant. The researchers' findings were recently published in

item.title: Excerpt: How to Close a Camp
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: immigration detention — Editor’s Note: The following is excerpted with permission from How to Close a Camp: Dispatches from the Fight Against Immigrant Detention, out from Haymarket Books this July. The Prison-Immigration Knot By the late 1980s, thanks to over a decade of laws criminalizing immigrants and expanding the camp system, prison and immigration detention industries were operating […] The post Excerpt: How to Close a Camp appeared first on The Texas Observer.

item.title: Trump fires warning shot at Canada: 'They need us to survive'
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — President Donald Trump once again took aim at Canada after reigniting his feud with America's neighbor to the north, claiming "they need us to survive" during an Oval Office event on Wednesday.Trump this week escalated his long-simmering feud with Canada after wildfires in the country blanketed much of the American Northeast and Midwest in dangerous levels of smoke, an occurrence becoming more common with the advance of climate change. Holding Canada responsible for the air quality threat, Trump

item.title: San Antonio filmmaker Jen Prince returns home with debut feature, message for women directors
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Jen Prince, a 1994 Taft High School graduate, will premiere her first feature film "Miles Underwater" at the 32nd Annual San Antonio Film Festival Wednesday night downtown at the Radius Center. San Antonio filmmaker Jen Prince returns home with debut feature, message for women directors was first posted on July 21, 2026 at 11:27 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: SAISD officially hires Adrian Bustillos as superintendent in split vote
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Bustillos will join SAISD from a Houston school district. He said he's ready to prevent the school district from a state takeover. SAISD officially hires Adrian Bustillos as superintendent in split vote was first posted on July 21, 2026 at 12:00 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: [$] Debating the role of large language models in the kernel community
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: large language model — Like many development communities, the kernel community has been struggling to determine how large language models will be used in its development process. The news has been dominated recently by a strongly worded missive from Linus Torvalds on the subject, but the discussion has been rather more wide-ranging and nuanced than that. Topics that have been considered recently include the LLM attribution requirement, code-review tools, dependence on proprietary tools, and whether there is a place

item.title: AI music platform Suno hits bum note as 55M users exposed in data breach, claims infosec expert
item.description: [Cybersecurity] Keyword: data breach — Have I Been Pwned confirms scale for first time

item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Burglary — Incident #SAPD-2026-0986490 | CENTRAL Division | 7/21/2026 8:13:02 AM

item.title: Triassic inferno: Fires raged across Europe for millennia during past global warming
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: global warming — The end-Triassic mass extinction, 201 million years ago, is linked to volcanism that resulted from the break-up of Pangea and which pumped out vast amounts of CO2 into the atmosphere, causing extreme global warming of 5 to 10 degrees Celsius. As a result, tree-dominated vegetation collapsed and the highly disturbed landscapes were colonized by ferns that formed savannahs across large swaths of what is now Northwest Europe.

item.title: How AI may drive union-resistant tech workers to the bargaining table
item.description: [Worker Cooperatives & Solidarity Economy] Keyword: collective bargaining — Tech workers are increasingly unionizing, trading Silicon Valley’s myth of exceptionalism for collective bargaining to contest the corporate deployment of artificial intelligenceFor decades, the technology industry was a fortress that labor unions couldn’t breach. Tech workers already had cushy compensation packages, dream benefits like unlimited vacation and free lunch, and a flat corporate hierarchy that made engineers feel as powerful as their bosses, all of whom dressed down in sneakers and

item.title: The Daily Reach and Recap have a whole new look: Sign up for our redesigned newsletters
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The refresh aims to make readers a more digestible experience as they engage with San Antonio Report content.The Daily Reach and Recap have a whole new look: Sign up for our redesigned newsletters was first posted on July 21, 2026 at 8:00 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: AI agent helps prepare synchrotron X-ray experimental measurements, paving the way for autonomous operation
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: AI agent — Artificial intelligence (AI) models are now used daily by many people worldwide, both for professional and personal purposes. Over the past decades, scientists specialized in various disciplines have also started using these models to conduct research or simplify their experimental practices.

item.title: Tribes and Environmental Groups Sue to Block Trump’s Gutting of the Endangered Species Act
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — This story was originally published by Inside Climate News and is reproduced here as part of the Climate Desk collaboration. Environmental groups and tribes are suing the Trump administration over a new rule eliminating the majority of habitat protections for imperiled wildlife protected by the Endangered Species Act.  Experts say the rule represents the most profound change to the law since it […]

item.title: Afghanistan: Deadly flash floods hit Nuristan province
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Some 20 people have been killed and more than 80 injured, with the city of Parun hit particularly hard. A lack of infrastructure and the effects of climate change have left the country vulnerable to natural disaster.

item.title: Indigenous knowledge key to building healthier and more sustainable food systems
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Traditional and Indigenous knowledge could play a vital role in addressing some of the world's most pressing challenges, including climate change, biodiversity loss and poor health and nutrition, according to a new review of current research.

item.title: VIA’s plans for Silver Line bus stops in historic districts head for review
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The Historic and Design Review Commission is scheduled to consider the plans August 5 and make a recommendation for eight of the 18 stations. VIA’s plans for Silver Line bus stops in historic districts head for review was first posted on July 21, 2026 at 5:00 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: Trump cuts to advisory groups leaves hospitals without key expertise on disease
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Under the Trump administration, dozens of advisory committees have been ended or frozen, including one on controlling infection in healthcare settings.

item.title: Global Value Chain Trade Reaches Historic Highs, Despite Push for Reshoring
item.description: [Worker Cooperatives & Solidarity Economy] Keyword: co-op — Despite rising geopolitical tensions, trade restrictions and calls to bring production back to domestic shores, the global economy remains more interconnected than it may appear. Trade through global value chains (GVCs) reached historic levels in 2024, according to a new report from the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD). GVCs, the cross-border production networks […]

item.title: Pentagon Slashed Civilian Harm Reduction Staff and Is Instead Using AI
item.description: [Opioid Treatment & Methadone] Keyword: harm reduction — CENTCOM, which oversees the war in Iran, has had its civilian harm reduction team slashed from 10 to just one.

item.title: Protect Your Privacy with California's DROP Tool
item.description: [Digital Rights & Privacy] Keyword: data broker — Are you a California resident? Then we've got exciting news for you: there's a tool just for you that lets you take a single, relatively easy step to protect your privacy. It's called a DROP request. (That's Delete Request and Opt-out Platform, if you're fancy). This one bit of paperwork lets you tell every data broker registered in the state of California that you'd like them to delete your information from their databases and request they stop selling and sharing your information. Here are s

item.title: Ancestral variation, not new mutations, may impact survival of species facing climate change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Research co-authored by an Oklahoma Museum of Natural History curator, her graduate student and an international team of scientists has shed new light on the evolutionary "toolkit" animals use to survive environmental change.

item.title: New web tool maps fossil locations across ancient continents in seconds
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Calculating a fossil's position throughout geological history is a complex process. In paleontology and other disciplines, determining a location in the past is a challenge that requires refined computational tools to process large volumes of data. Now, the BIOST3 Research Group at the University of Barcelona has designed an open-access web interface for the general public that simplifies this process and facilitates access to high-quality paleogeographic reconstructions.

item.title: TxDOT ramps up Operation Slowdown, speeding enforcement across Texas
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Operation Slowdown is back. Here’s why Texas drivers will see more speed enforcement and what TxDOT wants motorists to know.TxDOT ramps up Operation Slowdown, speeding enforcement across Texas was first posted on July 20, 2026 at 3:28 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. item.title: Frontier LLMs couldn't help Hugging Face fight off evil agents
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: LLM — Chinese open-weight model GLM 5.2 happily obliged

item.title: Move over quokkas—Rottnest's salt lake bacteria could be the island's next big claim to fame
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: genomics — Scientists at Murdoch University have read the full genetic code of bacteria from the salt lakes of Wadjemup (Rottnest Island), giving them their clearest picture yet of how these tiny organisms could be used to make biodegradable plastic. Their research is published in the journal Microbial Genomics.

item.title: Japan's first mosasaur premaxilla fossil hints at a new species
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — A research team from Okayama University of Science, Tokyo City University, the Natural History Museum, Kishiwada City (Osaka Prefecture), and other institutions has identified four previously unrecognized fossil bones from a rock containing mosasaur remains collected about 30 years ago in Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture.

item.title: NISD picks longtime employee Jessica Palomares as interim superintendent
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Jessica Palomares is the assistant superintendent of elementary education. She first joined NISD in 1996 as a special ed teacher. NISD picks longtime employee Jessica Palomares as interim superintendent was first posted on July 20, 2026 at 1:11 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: Civil rights groups sue Trump administration over childcare funding restrictions
item.description: [Government Accountability] Keyword: FOIA — HHS failed to respond to Foia request following claims of fraud against Somali childcare providers and DemocratsCivil rights groups have sued the US Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) after the agency failed to respond to a Freedom of Information Act request regarding the Trump administration’s restrictions on federal childcare and family assistance following claims of fraud against Somali childcare providers and Democratic state officials.The American Civil Liberties Union, the ACLU

item.title: Antibiotic-resistant bacteria increasingly leaving children vulnerable to common infections, global study finds
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: dialysis — Exclusive: Research spanning 82 countries finds antibiotic resistance increased in every region between 2004 and 2022Harry Booth has had a rough run through Australia’s coldest months. The 13-year-old has been in and out of hospital with a range of common chest and urinary tract infections that his doctors find challenging to treat.Harry was born with kidney failure and began dialysis at six months old, before receiving a kidney transplant from his father at age four. The surgery saved his life

item.title: New machine-learning tool improves accuracy of genomics research
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: genomics — University of Virginia School of Medicine scientists have identified a widespread source of error in a popular method for studying the genome and created a machine-learning tool to correct it. The free tool could improve the reliability of both conventional and single-cell data generated using this method, giving researchers a clearer view of how gene activity is controlled in health and disease and providing stronger foundations for future diagnostic and drug-development efforts.

item.title: Earth’s waters are quietly running out of oxygen, scientists warn
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: biodiversity loss — Oxygen is disappearing from oceans, lakes, rivers, and coastal waters at an alarming rate, threatening aquatic life and weakening the natural processes that help regulate Earth’s climate. Scientists warn that this widespread deoxygenation is closely connected to other major planetary threats, including warming, pollution, biodiversity loss, and ocean acidification.

item.title: Nearly 1,000 Wildfires in Canada Continue to Burn as Trump & Carney Double Down on Fossil Fuels
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Millions of people across North America have experienced hazardous air pollution this month as nearly a thousand active wildfires continue to rage in Canada. In addition to releasing hazardous chemicals and smoke darkening skies across the continent, the fires in Canada have also disproportionately impacted Indigenous communities, forcing thousands to evacuate their homes. Republican politicians have been loath to acknowledge the role of the climate crisis in making extreme weather events like t

item.title: Double murder accused strangled Devon cellmate while awaiting trial, jury told
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: LLM — James Desborough was convicted in March of murdering Steven Kempster, Winchester crown court jurors hearA fan of a TV show about serial killers strangled a cellmate in prison while awaiting trial for allegedly murdering two men and hiding their remains in Cornish woodland, a jury has heard.James Desborough, 40, is accused of killing Claudio Aquilino and Daniel Coleman and concealing parts of their bodies in the dense undergrowth close to his cabin near St Austell. Continue reading...

item.title: ‘It’s total devastation’: inside the Amazonian war against illegal gold mining
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — The Guardian goes on the ground with Brazilian forces fighting to eradicate mining that has destroyed protected areas of Indigenous landThe jerry-built bordello at the foot of Vila Cururu, an illegal gold-mining settlement at the heart of Brazil’s most desecrated Indigenous territory, was a hive of depravity until just a few weeks ago.Inebriated prospectors huddled around wooden tables, swigging beer and Scotch whisky, and ogling pole-dancing sex workers before going with them to shacks behind t

item.title: Pocket-size AI: Powerful phones star at China show
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: AI agent — Tech firms are racing to roll out advanced smartphones that use artificial intelligence to do everything from ordering food to composing messages upon a simple voice command. Wide adoption of phones running on so-called AI agents would be a revolution, but would also take control away from major apps, which aren’t always happy about it. […]

item.title: Expert reveals Trump's 'mortal blow' to vital endangered animals protections
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — The Trump administration this week released new rules pertaining to the Endangered Species Act, and as one nature photographer warned for MS NOW, it could very well be a "mortal blow" to the protections that have done much to bring certain animals back from the brink of extinction.Derrick Z. Jackson is an acclaimed and award-winning nature photographer, as well as a fellow in climate and energy at the Center for Science and Democracy's Union of Concerned Scientists. On Sunday, he published a new

item.title: National affordable housing builder plans first project in San Antonio despite market challenges
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — A national affordable housing developer is building 404 apartment units in San Antonio even as market challenges are slowing construction.National affordable housing builder plans first project in San Antonio despite market challenges was first posted on July 19, 2026 at 4:00 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. Please cont

item.title: Proposed EPA rule could weaken Texans’ voice on certain air permits used by data centers
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The change would give states greater power on deciding how much notice and public input residents receive before such permits are approved or denied.Proposed EPA rule could weaken Texans’ voice on certain air permits used by data centers was first posted on July 19, 2026 at 2:00 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. Please c

item.title: Burnham to say UK must be honest about its challenges as he prepares to become PM
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Labour leader will visit king to accept role on Monday and will start making cabinet appointments that afternoon Andy Burnham will say Britain must be honest about the challenges it faces when he becomes its sixth prime minister in a decade on Monday, as he prepares to introduce measures to ease the cost of living and allow more North Sea fossil fuel drilling.The Labour leader is expected to start making appointments in the afternoon after going to Buckingham Palace to accept the role of prime m
